t_en: Th—That's... fine. I didn't really care either way. I'm not that kind of person that cries about not adding a new friend. I would never do that!

t_ch: ...既然你这样薄情，就请别再打扰我了......
t_en: ...I see you are a fickle person. Please do not disturb me any further...

t_ch: 居然真有人能做到...
t_en: Someone really did it...

t_ch: 一次都没有输过...
t_en: Completely undefeated...

t_ch: 太强了...
t_en: That's amazing...

t_ch: 喂，什么时候能弄好啊？
t_en: Hey, when are you going to have that done?

t_ch: 呃...你是在跟我说话吗？
t_en: Uh... Are you talking to me?

t_ch: 是啊，我都在这里等多久了？快点把游戏奖励发给我。
t_en: Yes! I've been waiting here ages! Give me my game rewards.

t_ch: 可你刚才明明一直在看...
t_en: But this whole time you've just been staring at...

t_ch: 哟，好巧啊，没想到你也在这。
t_en: Oh! What a coincidence! I had no idea you'd be here, too!

t_ch: 现在这游戏真是一天比一天肝了。每次想拿奖励，都得来竞技场打上好久......
t_en: This game is getting more tedious by the day. Each time I want rewards, I have to spend ages fighting in the arena...

t_ch: 哎，我看你的以太灵练度好像都很高，有没有什么诀窍能传授一下？
t_en: Oh! I see your Aether Spirits are all really highly trained. Do you have any tips you can give me?

t_ch: 每天玩8小时就行了。
t_en: Just play for eight hours a day.

t_ch: 怎么听起来像上班一样...哦，我懂了，你肯定是靠着锲而不舍的精神才变得如此强大，对不对？
t_en: Why does that feel like a real job... Ah, I see! You mean that you only became this strong through hard work and perseverance, correct?

t_ch: 多么励志，多么伟大。我要学习这种精神才行。
t_en: So inspiring! So great! This is the spirit that I want to adopt!

t_ch: 你话题转换的好生硬...
t_en: You really know how to change a topic...

t_ch: 有吗？哦...我懂了，你肯定用的是非常高超的技巧，不能轻易让外人知道。
t_en: Really? Oh... I see! You must have used an extremely advanced technique that you can't just leak to outsiders!

t_ch: 像你这样的高手，一般玩家果然只能仰望、根本没办法超越...太棒了。
t_en: Regular players can only look up to you. There is no way for us to surpass a master such as yourself... You're amazing.

t_ch: 也请你继续努力下去吧。下届冠军赛，我还是看好你哟~
t_en: Please keep up your hard work. My money is still on you for next season~

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿...
t_en: Hehehe...

t_ch: 怎么看都不够...
t_en: It's just not enough...
Ahem...

t_ch: 哇啊！
t_en: Woah!

t_ch: 这...居然是你，哈哈...什么风把你吹到这儿来了？
t_en: It — I can't believe it's you, haha... What fair wind have you ridden in on?

t_ch: 之前我就想说，难道你是...
t_en: I wanted to ask you before. Are you...

t_ch: 什么？我是什么？
t_en: What? Am I what?

t_ch: 暗中关注我的粉丝？
t_en: A fan that follows me from the shadows?

t_ch: <size=36>哦哦哦哦哦哦哦！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ohhhhhhhh!</size>

t_ch: 没错，{NICKNAME}大人，我就是你最最忠实的粉丝啊！
t_en: That's right, great {NICKNAME}! I am your most loyal fan!

t_ch: 精神不正常的变态？
t_en: A strange pervert?

t_ch: <size=36>唔啊啊啊啊啊啊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Waaaaaaah!</size>

t_ch: 太伤心了！太难受了！我的情意为什么会被这样误会？！
t_en: I'm so hurt! I'm wounded! How could my affection be so badly misunderstood!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，我其实是你最最忠实的粉丝啊！
t_en: Great {NICKNAME}! I am actually your most loyal fan!

t_ch: 我一直在暗中注视你、仰望你、欣赏你击败对手时英武的身姿和冷漠的眼神！
t_en: I have always been watching you from the shadows, looking up to you, admiring your heroic stance and cold stare as you defeat your opponents!

t_ch: 每次看到你比赛中的完美表现，我都忍不住用镜头把一切记录下来——我家马桶盖上都贴着你的海报呢！
t_en: Each time I see you performing flawlessly in tournament, I can't help but record every moment on my camera. Even my toilet seat has your picture on it!

t_ch: 既然事已至此，那我就不再掩饰了...还请收下这个。它蕴含着我满满、满满的心意！
t_en: As you have already learned all this, there is no use in covering things up anymore... Please, take this. This is filled... absolutely filled to the brim with my love and admiration!

t_ch: 今后我也会一直看着你、支持你，直到这游戏关服的那天！
t_en: I will always keep watching you and supporting you, right up until the day that the servers close!

t_ch: 做好觉悟吧，{NICKNAME}大人！
t_en: Make sure you are prepared, great {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 哦，{NICKNAME}大人！你的出现奠定了我今天的好心情。
t_en: Oh, great {NICKNAME}! Your appearance always puts me in a wonderful mood for the day!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，看见你，我的胸膛就仿佛被击中了一样。
t_en: Great {NICKNAME}, every time I see you, it's like I've been struck in the chest.

t_ch: 我相信下次冠军也非你莫属，{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: I just know that the next championship belongs to you, great {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我会一直注视着你的，{NICKNAME}大人！
t_en: I am always watching you, great {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我是谁？我在哪儿？
t_en: Who am I? Where am I?

t_ch: 小家伙瑟瑟发抖，确认过你并无恶意后，从这一堆古怪的「包围圈」里走了出来。
t_en: The little creation is trembling. Once you're confirmed as not a threat, it walks out from its weird little "barricade."

t_ch: 你一棒子打碎了它牢固的「大门」，它看上去有些无所适从。
t_en: Considering you basically smashed down its "front doors" with your bat, it's at a loss about what to do.

t_ch: 我是谁？我在哪儿？等等……
t_en: Who am I? Where am I? Hang on...

t_ch: 你为什么要打烂我家的门？
t_en: Why are you smashing in the door to my home?

t_ch: …这是你家？
t_en: ...Is this your home?

t_ch: 算是吧，我才建好的。
t_en: Sure, I just built it.

t_ch: 抱歉，我只是顺手。
t_en: Sorry, I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 唔，这样吧，你也不是故意的。
t_en: Oh, I see. You didn't mean it.

t_ch: 我本来想盖一个大大的窝，让我的同伴一起过来住的。
t_en: I wanted to build this big den so all my friends could come and live there.

t_ch: 可这里越来越危险了，它们和我走散了，我想在这里等它们…可现在…塌房啦，我没有家了……
t_en: But it's getting dangerous over here, and they got separated from me. I want to wait for them here... but now... I don't have a home to go back to...

t_ch: 我可以送你回去。
t_en: I can take you back.

t_ch: 我知道它们在哪。
t_en: I know where they are.

t_ch: 真的？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: （为它指引回家的路）
t_en: (Point out the way home for it)

t_ch: 太好了，我终于有家了！
t_en: That's fantastic. Finally, a home of my own!

t_ch: 在你的指引下，小家伙回到了它温暖的新家。
t_en: With your guidance, the little creation made it back to its cozy new home.

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜……
t_en: *Snore*... *Snore*...

t_ch: 实验田里有奇怪的呼噜声，这令你想起在空间站的某处角落，生长着会说话的植物的奇妙经历。
t_en: A strange snoring is coming from inside the experimental plant nurseries, which reminds you of a wondrous encounter with a talking plant elsewhere in the space station.

t_ch: 但这里显然没有什么植物，目光所及之处，只有半死不活的杂草。
t_en: But obviously there are no plants here — only half-dead weeds as far as the eye can see.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 做好事，徒手拔杂草！
t_en: Do a good deed, and pull out the weeds with your bare hands!

t_ch: 尽管科员们暂时撤离了这里，但放任杂草长满实验田也不好。你热心地伸出双手，就地开始拔草。
t_en: The researchers may have been evacuated from here, but ignoring weeds in the experimental plant nurseries is no good. You enthusiastically reach out both hands to get started on the weeding process.

t_ch: 咕…呼噜？
t_en: Snort... *snooze*?

t_ch: 你好像拽住了一个沉甸甸的物体，摸上去还有些温热。
t_en: You seem to have pulled at a heavy object. It feels quite warm to the touch, too.

t_ch: 你使劲往上拽，一个意想不到的家伙挣扎着推开你的手，可怜巴巴地看着你。
t_en: You give it an almighty yank, and out of the blue this little thing pushes your hand away before looking up at you with puppy dog eyes.

t_ch: 什么也不做。
t_en: Do nothing.

t_ch: 好痛…我的发夹都要被你拽掉了……
t_en: Ow-wee... You almost pulled out my hair clip...

t_ch: 抱歉，我以为是杂草。
t_en: Sorry, I thought these were all weeds.

t_ch: …我头上可不会长杂草。喏，这是花，我最喜欢的漂亮小花。
t_en: ...I don't have any weeds growing on my head. Look! These are flowers. My favorite little pretty flowers.

t_ch: 你是怎么冒出来的？
t_en: Where did you come from?

t_ch: …这话不该我问你吗？我在这儿睡得好好的，突然就被你拽起来。
t_en: ...Shouldn't I be the one asking you that? I was sleeping nice and snugly right here before you suddenly tugged me up.

t_ch: 这里的土壤很肥沃，有棵好心的花花跟我说，躺在这上面头顶就会长出漂亮的花来。
t_en: The soil here is very fertile, and a kind-hearted flower told me that pretty flowers would grow out of my head if I just lay here.

t_ch: 你看我头顶这个，是不是很漂亮！
t_en: Check out the top of my head. Isn't it beautiful!

t_ch: 这好像是四叶草。
t_en: I think that's a four-leaf clover.

t_ch: 是会带来好运的小花，不是野草噢。
t_en: They're flowers that can bring you luck — not weeds.

t_ch: 花…跟你说话？
t_en: Flowers... can talk to you?

t_ch: 对呀，你会说话，我也会说话，花花草草会说话很正常呀。
t_en: Of course. You can talk, I can talk, so isn't it totally normal for flowers to talk, too?

t_ch: 嗯，确实漂亮。
t_en: Mmm, it really is beautiful.

t_ch: 是吧，它好好看！
t_en: Right? It's beautiful!

t_ch: 对了，我这是睡了多久…怎么只剩下我一个了，我的小伙伴呢？
t_en: That's right. How long have I been out for... Why am I the only one left? Where are my little friends?

t_ch: 它们都回家了嘛？呜呜，完了，我不认路，这下怎么回去啊！
t_en: Have they all gone home? *sob* It's game over. I don't know the way back. How am I going to get home!

t_ch: （为它指引回家的路）
t_en: (Point out the way home for it)

t_ch: 噢，你们搞了个新家啊，我这就去，谢谢你呀。
t_en: Oh, you've got a new home all ready. I'll go over right now. Thank you.

t_ch: 这个小礼物就送给你吧，有空的话，请帮我回来看望下这些花花草草吧，它们很可爱的。
t_en: Here's a little something for you. If you ever get the chance, please come back and help me look after the plants here. They're all really cute.

t_ch: 唔，现在的话还是算了，它们在睡觉，咱们小点声，别吵醒它们。
t_en: Oh, forget it for now. They're asleep, so let's keep the noise down and try not to wake them.

t_ch: 在你的指引下，小家伙回到了它温暖的新家。
t_en: With your guidance, the little creation made it back to its cozy new home.

t_ch: 实验田很安静，没有「阮•梅」造物在此酣睡，只有杂草丛生。
t_en: The experimental plant nurseries are very quiet and none of Ruan Mei's creations are here — just weeds covering the ground.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 好事做尽，拔掉杂草。
t_en: Keep doing good deeds, and pull out the weeds.

t_ch: 你弯下腰，流了不少汗，一番辛勤的体力劳作后，什么也没有发生。但你还是觉得很充实，或许这就是所谓的精神满足感吧。
t_en: You bend over and after lots of heavy sweating from a burst of physical labor — nothing happens. But you still feel very fulfilled. Maybe this is what people mean by something being spiritually satisfying.

t_ch: 你正在擦手，准备转身离开这里。一阵窸窣声惊动了你，但对方消失得极快，就连你也抓不住它的踪迹。
t_en: Just as you wipe down your hands and prepare to go, a rustling noise startles you — but they are disappearing so fast that not even you are able to tell where they went.

t_ch: 你回过神检查，这才发现——不知是何方神圣在你口袋里塞了一把「大自然的馈赠」。
t_en: You come back to your senses only to discover that somehow, someone has stuffed some of "nature's gift" into your pocket.

t_ch: 默默旁观。
t_en: Observe in silence.

t_ch: 你选手袖手旁观，看看是否会有传说中的「会说话的植物」出现，但很可惜，什么都没有发生。
t_en: You watch on one side to see whether or not these mystical "talking plants" show up, but unfortunately nothing happens.

t_ch: 实验田里一如既往地安静，你努力寻找着杂草的踪迹，但很可惜，在你的连日劳作下，它们已不复存在。
t_en: The experimental plant nurseries remains as quiet as ever. You try hard to find traces of the weeds but, alas, after days of hard work they no longer exist.

t_ch: 有什么东西轻轻敲了敲你的脚后跟，你转过身望去，又是大自然的慷慨。
t_en: You feel something tapping on your heel. You turn around to see once more the generosity of nature.

t_ch: 你捡起这一切，准备离去，耳畔响起一道细小的声音——
t_en: You pick it all up and are about to leave, when you hear a tiny voice inside your ear...

t_ch: 「谢谢你…我们要冬眠啦，拜拜！」是谁在说话，是你的幻觉吗？这一切如此费解。
t_en: "Thank you... We're off to hibernate now. Bye bye!" Who's that talking, now... Are you hallucinating? Everything seems so baffling.

t_ch: 永仁……
t_en: Yongren...

t_ch: 快递箱里不知存放着何物，你远远地就看到箱子在抖动，抖动的频率渐渐微弱了下来。
t_en: You notice an express box in the distance shaking, though the frequency of its shuddering movements are slowly diminishing. What's inside the box remains a mystery to you.

t_ch: 你正准备拆开箱子看看，指尖便被一阵冰冷的金属物体轻轻拍了拍，你歪头望去，里面竟是一只奄奄一息的机巧鸟！
t_en: As you open the box, you feel the sensation of cold, metallic material against your fingertips. Upon closer inspection, you discover a cycrane on the brink of a complete breakdown!

t_ch: 阿仁…的…证……
t_en: Certificate... Yon...

t_ch: 「阿仁」是谁？
t_en: What do you mean by "Yon"?

t_ch: 云骑…永仁……
t_en: Yongren... the Cloud Knight...

t_ch: 「证」是什么证？
t_en: What do you mean by "certificate"?

t_ch: 永仁…证明……
t_en: The proof... for Yongren...

t_ch: …快！快去！救…救永仁！
t_en: ...Quick! Go! Save... save Yongren!

t_ch: 机巧鸟拍打翅膀的动静越发微弱，在即将停下的刹那，它的眼珠陡然瞪大，不住地向你求救。
t_en: The cycrane's wings are fluttering weakly. Just as its movement is about to stop, it suddenly widens its eyes and gasps for help.

t_ch: 机巧鸟在说话间整只鸟身为之一振，像是受到了极大的鼓舞。这句话才刚说完，它的鸟头便「啪」地一声倒地，任你如何触摸也不再有回应。
t_en: The cycrane speaks with great strain, as if it knows its borrowed time is running out. Just as it finishes its words, the cycrane's head greets the ground with a resounding crash, and now it no longer responds to your touch.

t_ch: 在它彻底倒下的瞬间，几张单薄的纸页从箱子缝隙中滑落，你连忙捡了起来——
t_en: In the instance of its collapse, thin sheets of paper slip from the cracks of the box, and you quickly pick them up.

t_ch: 这东西好像是你的……
t_en: I think these are yours...

t_ch: 我的？我的什么东西？
t_en: Mine? What do you mean?

t_ch: 没想到这东西竟然落到了你手里，此事涉及我云骑军的机密，你还是跟我走一趟吧。
t_en: I didn't expect you to have those documents. Well, this is highly confidential Cloud Knight information, and now I need you to come with me.

t_ch: 这就要抓我了？
t_en: Are you going to arrest me?

t_ch: 不，不是要抓你，恰恰相反，你做得很好…只是，这件事不能走漏风声……
t_en: Arrest? No, not at all. You've done an excellent job... but this must be kept a secret...

t_ch: 什么机密？我完全不知情啊！
t_en: Highly confidential? But I don't know anything!

t_ch: 你是否知情都没关系，这份文件…我带你走只是想让我的同僚保证你的人身安全……
t_en: It doesn't matter if you know the details or not. This document... Well, I'm only bringing you along so that my colleague can guarantee your safety...

t_ch: 等等，我预感这件事会很麻烦…
t_en: Hold on. I'm already smelling trouble...

t_ch: 我明白了，我会等你，就等一小会，你快些决定跟不跟我走。
t_en: I see. I'll wait. Just for a short while though. Hurry. Decide whether you want to come with me.

t_ch: 咱们动身吧！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: …接下的事情会很麻烦，我要带走你只是确保你的安全，同时也是确保我的安全。
t_en: ...What we do next might be quite annoying. I'm bringing you along just to ensure your safety and as well as mine.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下…
t_en: I need to think about it...

t_ch: 我明白了，我会等你，就等一小会，你快些决定跟不跟我走。
t_en: I see. I'll wait. Just for a short while though. Hurry. Decide whether you want to come with me.

t_ch: 咱们动身吧！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: 奇怪，接头的线人，怎么不见了，我…我明明通知过他。
t_en: Wait, where is my contact? I... I definitely informed him about my arrival.

t_ch: …难道……
t_en: ...Could it be...?

t_ch: 糟了！一定是出事了，听着，我本想让我的同僚来保障你的安全，但他却不在这里，「上面」一定是出事了！
t_en: No... Something is terribly wrong! Look, I was going to ask my colleague to keep you safe, but he's not where he's supposed to be. Something must have gone wrong with the "higher-ups"!

t_ch: 待会儿就躲在杂物堆后面千万别出来…等我喊你再出来，万万不可轻举妄动，听清楚了吗？
t_en: Just stay behind the boxes and don't come out... unless I tell you to. Don't do anything stupid. Are we clear?

t_ch: 收到！
t_en: Yeah, sure!

t_ch: 千万别轻举妄动！
t_en: Just... don't do anything stupid!

t_ch: 放心吧，我能保护自己。
t_en: Don't worry. I can take care of myself.

t_ch: 哎！你是不知道那帮人有多凶狠，总之还是听我的，别轻举妄动！
t_en: Listen, you have no idea how ruthless they can be. Anyway, just listen to me and don't do anything stupid!

t_ch: 跟紧我！
t_en: Stick with me!

t_ch: 永仁仔细地将你护在身后，将你带到了一处隐秘的地方让你躲了起来。在确保自己足够安全后，你这才敢侧身观察永仁……
t_en: Yongren places himself in front of you to shield you from danger, then leads you to a hidden spot and tells you to hide there. Once you are sure of safety, only then you dare to turn to the side and observe Yongren closely...

t_ch: 我就说诚哥怎么不在「老地方」…果然是你干…果然是你！你就是那个「内鬼」！
t_en: No wonder Brother Cheng wasn't in his usual spot... It was you... You are the mole!

t_ch: 你不要这么激动。你口口声声说我是「内鬼」，真是奇了怪了，你不也是个卧底吗？你说，咱俩都是卧底，你怎么就对我敌意这么大？
t_en: Calm down. You're saying I'm a mole, but aren't you undercover, too? Why so hostile against me when we're both moles?

t_ch: 我跟你不一样，我行得正走得正。
t_en: No, I'm nothing like you. I've been a servant of justice.

t_ch: 你们神策府的人还挺有意思，一开口就不对味，但一个个都挺自信的。
t_en: It's funny how you folks from the Seat of Divine Foresight never seem to fit in with the more dicey crowd, yet all believe you fit in so well.

t_ch: 我查过了，你一个普普通通的底层云骑，外派卧底多年，在神策府连份档案都没有，说话口气还这么大，我真的很好奇，是景元将军给你的自信吗？
t_en: I've looked into your background. You're just a low-level Cloud Knight who has been undercover for years and doesn't even have an official file on record at the Seat of Divine Foresight. What makes you talk with such confidence?

t_ch: 少废话，我本以为你是兄弟，可没想到你个浓眉大眼的竟背叛了大家…你对得起被「药王秘传」残党所害的兄弟姐妹吗？！
t_en: Save your breath! I thought we were brothers, but you betrayed everyone... Can you face our fallen brothers and sisters who died at the hands of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus with a clear conscience?

t_ch: 我说了，你不要这么激动嘛，我今天来，是想跟你谈合作的。你我相识多年，手足一场，给个机会呗。
t_en: As I said, let's calm down. We've known each other for years, and I'm here to talk about us collaborating. Just give me a chance.

t_ch: 什么机会？
t_en: What chance?

t_ch: 以前我没得选，是他们威胁我，我才不得已加入了云骑军。可现在，「药王秘传」的事已经败露，我倒是想做个好人……
t_en: I didn't have a choice before. They threatened me, so I had to join the Cloud Knights as an undercover agent. But now, the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus have been exposed, so I want to take this chance to do the right thing...

t_ch: 好啊，那你和神策府的策士们说，看他们让不让你做个好人。
t_en: Yeah, sure, you can tell that to the counselors at the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 兄弟一场，给条活路。
t_en: Come on, friend. Just give me a way out. Please.

t_ch: 对不起，我是云骑！
t_en: I'm sorry, but I'm a Cloud Knight!

t_ch: 档案和证明都没有，谁知道你是云骑？
t_en: Hah! You don't even have official documents! Who'd believe that you're truly a Cloud Knight?

t_ch: 这不可能，诚哥他手上的——
t_en: But I do. Brother Cheng has my documents...

t_ch: 他的保密机巧鸟和玉兆在我手上，那几份文件，自然也……嘿嘿！
t_en: This is where you're mistaken. His encrypted cycrane and jade abacus are now in my hands, along with your documents... Hehe!

t_ch: 今天我定要替云骑军上下除掉你这个叛徒！
t_en: You filthy traitor! I'll take you down today for the sake of the Cloud Knights!

t_ch: 你以为只有你会云骑的技艺吗？已经跟你说过了，给个机会嘛，可你既然不听，那就由不得你——
t_en: Please. You think you're the only one trained in the Cloud Knights' martial arts here? I've pleaded with you for a chance to do the right thing, but if you won't listen, then I guess I'll have to...

t_ch: （上去制止建明）
t_en: (Stop Jian Ming.)

t_ch: 你是？
t_en: Who are you?
You...

t_ch: 是景元将军派我来的。
t_en: General Jing Yuan sent me.

t_ch: 都老实点，我是景元。
t_en: I'm Jing Yuan. Behave yourselves!

t_ch: 胡说八道…我有印象，你和那位太卜曾经一起并肩而行，估计是有些地位。
t_en: Nonsense... but I have seen you walking alongside the master diviner, so I suppose you do have some status.

t_ch: 难怪是个生面孔…既然如此，也好，既然有外人在，阿仁，就当是有个见证人，这事窝在我心里很久了，无论如何，今天都得做出个了断了！
t_en: No wonder I've never met you before... In that case, brother Yong, I gotta get something off my chest that's been bothering me for years! This matter must be settled, one way or another!

t_ch: 听着——我说过了，以前我没得选，是他们用家人威胁我，我不得已才加入云骑军当什么「卧底」。
t_en: Listen, I told you: I didn't have a choice. The Disciples threatened my family, so I had to join the Cloud Knights as their "mole."

t_ch: 可我从未没伤害过同袍，我还用这一手「药王秘传」内授的医术救了不少人——还有你，你那开药摊的经验也是我传授给你的。
t_en: But I never hurt my comrades. I even used the techniques from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus to save many knights. And you! I even taught you my pharmaceutical skills.

t_ch: 我一早知道阿仁你是卧底，你以为就凭你那么烂的医术功底，是怎么在他们眼皮子底下没被发现的？还不是我替你遮掩去了！
t_en: I knew you were a mole from the beginning. You really think you managed to evade their notice right under their noses with your incredible feats of "espionage," brother Yong? Come on, it's me who'd been covering for you! Your medical knowledge was terrible!

t_ch: 十年了！他们跟我说，十年之后便可功成身退。说好了十年，十年之后又十年，这都多久了，我实在是受够了！比起听从他们使唤，我更想当个堂堂正正的云骑军！
t_en: It's been ten years! They promised me I'd be free after ten years! But now ten years have passed, and they've asked for another ten! I'm done! I've had enough! I'd rather be a true Cloud Knight than a pawn for them!

t_ch: 阿仁，给个机会我，我想做个好人……
t_en: Please, just give me a chance to do the right thing, Yong...

t_ch: 阿仁，让他做个好人吧…
t_en: You should give him a chance, Yong...

t_ch: 好啊，去跟神策府说啊…
t_en: Yeah, you can tell that to the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 我看你是痴心妄想！你既已伤害了诚哥，怎么还好意思要我放过你？我是放过你了，可你有放过诚哥吗？
t_en: This is ridiculous! You hurt Brother Cheng, and now you're asking me to show you mercy? Why didn't you show him any mercy!?

t_ch: 我真的没对诚哥出手，只不过将诚哥的机巧鸟和玉兆「借」走了。
t_en: I didn't hurt him at all. I just "borrowed" his cycrane and jade abacus.

t_ch: 你当我傻吗？
t_en: You think I'm a fool?

t_ch: 哼，想来你也不信，咱们来做一笔交易。你加入云骑军的证明文件在我手上，你答应不揭发我是卧底的秘密，我就把文件还你。否则……
t_en: *sigh* You just won't believe me, will you? Alright, how about we make a deal? As you see, I have your official documents. You promise not to expose my identity as an undercover agent, and I'll give those documents back to you. Otherwise...

t_ch: 文件？我已经交给永仁了。
t_en: Yongren's official documents? But I've already given them back to him.

t_ch: 什么？可恶，难道是那只机巧鸟擅自…等等，你不会是真的想做掉我吧？
t_en: What? You... Did that cycrane just... Wait, you're not really going to kill me, are you?

t_ch: （等待永仁的决定）
t_en: (Wait for Yongren to make his decision.)

t_ch: 否则什么？你倘若真想做个好人，你还会耍这些手段？
t_en: Otherwise what? Would someone who wants to "do the right thing" use such underhanded methods?

t_ch: 啧，好吧，我会直接把证明文件给你，我想做个好人，没有任何隐瞒。
t_en: Tsk, fine. I'll give back your documents. As I said, I just want to do the right thing. I got nothing to hide about that.

t_ch: 卧底的滋味，这些年来，想必你也清楚…我实在不想再过每天那种担惊受怕的日子了…「药王秘传」的线报我也全部上传回去了……
t_en: You know better than anyone what it's like to be a mole... I just don't want to live in fear anymore... I've sent in all the information I know about the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus and done my part...

t_ch: 报告里我说了是你提供的线报，想来你的任务也算完成，回去也是指日可待的事了。
t_en: In my report, I stated that it was you who provided the tip-offs. I suppose that means your mission is complete, and you'll be back in the Cloud Knights in no time.

t_ch: 只要你不说，我不说…我们都能好好的，我去做个好人，而你很快就会结束卧底生涯。
t_en: As long as we keep it a secret... we'll both be fine. I'll have a chance to do the right thing, and you won't need to stay undercover any longer.

t_ch: 今天有外人见证，我答应你。但回去之后我要好好查探下诚哥和其他信息，如若属实，我自然会替你保密。可要是……
t_en: Alright, I promise you. But once I return, I'll verify Cheng's situation and the other details. If what you said is true, I'll keep your secrets to myself and nobody else. However, if...

t_ch: 你放心！绝对属实！我再也不想当卧底了！
t_en: Don't worry! What I said is the absolute truth! I never want to be a mole ever again!

t_ch: 那好，还请{NICKNAME}不要外传！我俩尚有急事要处理，暂且告别，神策府种种，今日倒是叫你见笑了……
t_en: And please keep this between us, {NICKNAME}. I'm sorry for getting you involved in all this trouble with the Seat of Divine Foresight. Now, if you'll excuse us...

t_ch: 你目送永仁和建明一并离去，你不知道他们之间到底经历了什么，又为何如此。
t_en: You watch as Yongren and Jian Ming leave together. You find yourself wondering what they experienced together and why all of that happened.

t_ch: 但似乎原本难解的局面因你的出现而发生了改变，这是永仁也未曾想到的。
t_en: However, the originally unsolvable dilemma has changed thanks to your intervention. It was something that Yongren didn't expect.

t_ch: 还有几册书没有找到……
t_en: Hmmm... Some tomes are still missing...

t_ch: 请问是需要找回什么书吗？
t_en: Do you need to retrieve any books?

t_ch: 对了，你不是上次来找《要药分剂》的人吗？上次我有帮到你，请问你能帮帮我吗？
t_en: Oh, aren't you the one who asked for the Compendium of Remedies last time? I helped you last time, so could you do me a favor in return?

t_ch: …稍等一下，丹鼎司有几册书丢失了，偏偏是几本很麻烦的书。丹枢大人什么信息也没有留给我，我要怎么找回来……
t_en: ...Well, some books have gone missing from the Alchemy Commission, and, well, they're essentially some very troublesome texts. That, and Lady Dan Shu hasn't left me any clues. How am I supposed to get them back?...

t_ch: …唉，我又忘了，现在不该称她为大人了。你找我有什么事吗？
t_en: ...Ah, I forgot again. I shouldn't call her "Lady" now. So, what brings you here?

t_ch: 有什么我能帮的忙吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can help with?

t_ch: 不怕你这样的化外民笑话，丹鼎司内除了医典专著之外，还封存着一些「禁忌书典」。它们原本应该是小心保存的，如今却不小心「失窃」了。
t_en: Please forgive me for this awkward situation. Actually, in addition to medical texts, we also keep some "forbidden tomes" here in the Alchemy Commission. They are supposed to be carefully preserved, but now they've been stolen.

t_ch: 我猜跟药王秘传这次事件也有些干系，不过，我问过神策府方面，他们目前还没有找回「禁忌书典」的消息，这让我实在是有些头疼。
t_en: I guess the theft has something to do with the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus as well. I've asked the Seat of Divine Foresight, but they haven't found any clues about the forbidden tomes. It's turning into a real headache for me.

t_ch: 我看你那震惊的表情就知道，你肯定想问「丹鼎司到底行不行啊」。你现在明白了吧，让我回来继任丹士长可以说是危机重重了。
t_en: Oh, by your astonished expression, I can tell that you actually want to say "Come on! Has the Alchemy Commission ever done anything right!?" Well, now you see how perilous my position as the chief alchemist is.

t_ch: 丹鼎司为什么有「禁忌书典」？
t_en: Why is the Alchemy Commission keeping those forbidden tomes?

t_ch: 因为这些书典在某种程度上算得上是「医书」，或者说它们本身就是一种「治疗手段」——只要阅读它，就发生不可思议的现象。
t_en: Because these tomes are also medical texts — in a sense, they themselves are a treatment method. Just by reading them, patients will experience miraculous things.

t_ch: 有些人阅读了「治疗失眠」的医书长梦不起；相对的，有些人阅读了「不眠不休」的医书之后，再也没能入睡……
t_en: For example, some patients who read the "Cure for Insomnia" fall asleep for a long time, while others who read "Fight Sleepiness" never fall asleep again...

t_ch: 那岂不是很危险？
t_en: Aren't they super dangerous?

t_ch: 只要读不懂，书典就还是安全的。
t_en: As long as you don't understand their contents, they should be safe.

t_ch: 这种东西趁早销毁比较好…
t_en: You should destroy such dangerous books before it's too late...

t_ch: 丹鼎司内也有一些主张销毁「禁忌书典」的医士…不过这书既然是药，就有着两面性，关键得看医生如何用药。
t_en: Some healers in the Alchemy Commission also advocate for the destruction of the forbidden tomes just like you... but in my opinion, since those tomes have medicinal value, they can be really helpful if they end up in the right hands.

t_ch: 听上去像空间站的奇物？
t_en: Sounds like the Curios in the space station, don't you agree?

t_ch: 你是说天才俱乐部的那位，我听说她的空间站也收藏了一些奇怪的东西。
t_en: You mean the one from the Genius Society? Yeah, I heard she's got some unusual collectibles on her space station.

t_ch: …某种意义上来说，丹鼎司也在做类似的事情，不过我们更专注医学领域。
t_en: ...In a way, the Alchemy Commission is doing the same thing, but we're more specialized in the field of medicine.

t_ch: 「禁忌书典」存在着「阅读门槛」，对大多数人来说，那些书典是无害的。
t_en: There is a certain "reading barrier" for those forbidden tomes, so they are completely harmless to the majority of people.

t_ch: 可是一旦流传在外，很难说有「擅长解读之人」会遭遇什么事情，还得尽快回收。
t_en: However, if they fall into the hands of someone who is skilled at deciphering them... it pains me to imagine the terrible things that could happen. They must be retrieved as soon as possible.

t_ch: 就这样告诉我，没有关系吗？
t_en: Is it okay to spill your secrets like this?

t_ch: 没有关系，丹鼎司内知道「禁忌书典」的人不少，比你的嘴巴更松的人也有的是。
t_en: Don't worry. A lot of members in the Alchemy Commission know about the forbidden tomes, and some of them have a reputation for having loose lips.

t_ch: 纯粹依靠丹鼎司医士们的情报网，想要把这些书典全部收回颇有难度，我也是听神策府的青镞大人引荐，决定将这件事拜托给你。
t_en: Retrieving these tomes won't be easy if we rely solely on our own intelligence network. That's why I need your help with this. After all, it is Madam Qingzu from the Seat of Divine Foresight who recommended you to me.

t_ch: 更重要的是，我需要你的帮助，因此我认为开诚布公是我应尽的义务。如果说不清楚，这对你来说也不方便是吧？
t_en: Since I need your help, it's my duty to tell you everything I know about this issue, otherwise it'd be a hassle, right?

t_ch: 我也不一定能找到…
t_en: I can't guarantee that I'll find them...

t_ch: 没事，收回书典本身就是一件「没有头绪的事情」，我也不指望短时间内能够全部收回。毕竟药王秘传和丹枢大人究竟做了什么，我们无从得知。
t_en: Don't worry. I understand how tough this task is, and I'm not expecting you to bring them back anytime soon. We have no clue what Lady Dan Shu or the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus did, after all.

t_ch: 这件事就交给我吧。
t_en: I see. I'll handle it.

t_ch: 太好了，青镞大人听说了丹鼎司的困境之后，建议将这个事情委托给你，我当时还有些半信半疑的，现在想来，青镞大人果然是没有看错人。
t_en: Awesome! I wasn't sure if you'd help me when Madam Qingzu recommended you, but now I can see that she was right to have faith in you!

t_ch: 还有三本书要找，分别是《开智之卷》、《无言之卷》、以及《瘈狂之卷》。丹鼎司这边资料有限，你有什么想了解的吗？
t_en: So, you need to retrieve these three scrolls: The Manuscript of Wisdom, the Manuscript of Silence, and the Manuscript of Delirium. While I don't have many leads on them, I'll do my best to answer any questions you might have.

t_ch: 《开智之卷》？
t_en: The Manuscript of Wisdom?

t_ch: 曾有不识字之人阅读此书。谁也不知道他究竟看了什么，看完之后智力飞涨，虽然未及天才俱乐部成员，也已远胜常人。
t_en: Once, an illiterate person read this book and suddenly became incredibly intelligent. Although they weren't on par with the members of the Genius Society, they became way smarter than ordinary people.

t_ch: 《无言之卷》？
t_en: The Manuscript of Silence?

t_ch: 曾有盲眼之人阅读此书。据该患者称，看完这本书之后，能听见「无言之声」，但是没人知道这「无言之声」来自哪里。
t_en: Once, a blind person read this book and claimed that they could hear the Sound of Silence, although no one knows exactly where such Sound of Silence comes from.

t_ch: 《瘈狂之卷》？
t_en: The Manuscript of Delirium?

t_ch: 曾有狂妄之人阅读此书。但此人只翻开这本书看了一眼，便双目圆瞪、全身痉挛、不省人事了。
t_en: Once, a reckless person tried to read this book, but as soon as they opened it, they started convulsing and then passed out.

t_ch: 等我找到了，再来找你吧。
t_en: I see. I'll come back to you once I find them all.

t_ch: 那就拜托你了。
t_en: I leave it in your capable hands.

t_ch: 咪咪你怎么了？
t_en: What's going on, Mimi?

t_ch: 我已不再是从前的咪咪。
t_en: I'm no longer the same Mimi I used to be.

t_ch: 咪咪，你怎么了？你正常一点好不好，我真的有点害怕了。
t_en: What happened to you, Mimi? Can you just act normal? You're starting to freak me out, you know?

t_ch: 都说了让你先别说话，鑫鑫，我只是正在思考现状罢了。
t_en: Hold on, Xinxin. I'm still trying to figure out the current situation.

t_ch: 谛听会说话诶！
t_en: Wait, this Diting can actually talk!?

t_ch: 唉哟，别说你了，连我也吓了一跳…这谛听跟了我四十年了，突然就开始说话了……
t_en: Yeah, I'm just as astonished as you are... This Diting has served me for forty years, and now it suddenly starts talking...

t_ch: 你管它叫咪咪？
t_en: Did you call it "Mimi"?

t_ch: 鑫鑫，有人目击到你在和一只谛听说话，然后第一反应是「你给它起名叫咪咪？」由此可见「咪咪」这个名字是真的不适合我。
t_en: You see, Xinxin? Someone saw you talking to your Diting and their first response was "Did you call it Mimi?" So, I guess we can say that the name "Mimi" doesn't really suit me.

t_ch: 我觉得还挺可爱的啊…哎呀，现在不是聊这个的时候……
t_en: But I thought it was a cute name... Oh, let's forget about it for now...

t_ch: 我知道你，你不是{NICKNAME}吗？我听说你是见多识广的化外民，你知道这是怎么回事吗？
t_en: Hey, I know you! You're {NICKNAME}, right? I've heard that you're a knowledgeable Outworlder, so maybe you happen to know why my Diting has suddenly started talking?

t_ch: 这些我从药王秘传手里缴获的赃物，其中有个叫《开智之卷》的小册子，一共没几页，上面尽是些鬼画符。
t_en: Here's the thing: I confiscated some stolen property from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, and among them was a booklet called the Manuscript of Wisdom with all sorts of weird symbols on it.

t_ch: 我也不知道这东西是什么，就想让咪咪嗅探一下。结果咪咪翻了翻这个小册子，突然双手抱胸原地坐下了，还在连连叹气……
t_en: I didn't know what the text was, so I asked Mimi to sniff it for any scents. But as soon as Mimi started flipping through the pages, it suddenly sat down and started sighing...

t_ch: 咪咪也是四十多年的老谛听了，容易出故障，嘴里一直嘀咕着「人类真蠢啊…」，我也是第一次听见咪咪张口说人话。
t_en: I mean... Mimi's a forty-year-old Diting by this point. Glitches and bugs aren't uncommon in it. It kept muttering "Humans are so stupid..." That's the first time I've ever heard it speak using a human tongue!

t_ch: 行啦，这个事情也犯不着{F#她}{M#他}来给你解释。况且{F#她}{M#他}也未必搞得清楚。
t_en: Well, I don't think we need {F#her}{M#him} to explain what's going on. Perhaps not even {F#she}{M#he} knows what has truly transpired.

t_ch: 那本《开智之卷》上的鬼画符本质是一种逻辑回路，仅仅通过视觉传导就完善了我的基础功能……
t_en: The "weird symbols" on the Manuscript of Wisdom are actually a logical circuit that enlightens its readers through visual transmission alone...

t_ch: 对于你们这些能识文断字的人来说，「符号」会被赋予特定的意义，反而没有办法被《开智之卷》启迪智慧。
t_en: Literate people tend to give meanings to symbols, which will prevent them from truly understanding the contents within the Manuscript of Wisdom.

t_ch: 对我来说，这智慧突如其来，现在我已经想到了一百种毁灭世界的办法了……
t_en: Thanks to this sudden enlightenment, now I've come up with <unbreak>100</unbreak> ways to destroy this world...

t_ch: 看来，我不得不消灭你了。
t_en: Looks like I'll have to destroy you.

t_ch: 你能不能先思考再动手。
t_en: Can't you just think before you act?

t_ch: 除非你告诉我，我才会相信。
t_en: I won't believe your words until you tell me what they are.

t_ch: 省省力气吧，我不会告诉你的。
t_en: Save your breath. I'm not telling you anything.

t_ch: 我已经太聪明了，毁灭世界在我看来毫无意义。只有那些反物质军团的造物、泯灭帮的蠢货才想要做这样的事情…换言之，我是不屑于去做的。
t_en: I'm simply too smart to do something meaningless like destroying the world. Only the morons from the Antimatter Legion or the Annihilation Gang would do such a foolish thing... In short, it's beneath me to destroy the world. Absolutely not.

t_ch: 那…那你说你变聪明了，你变得有多聪明了呢？要不，{NICKNAME}你出个题吧？
t_en: B—But how smart have you become? Maybe you could ask it a question, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 一加一等于几？
t_en: What's one plus one?

t_ch: 你这问题普通的谛听也能算得出来。
t_en: Even an ordinary Diting could answer such a simple question.

t_ch: 该怎么说呢…总觉得能意识到这个问题很愚蠢，已经不是普通谛听能做到的事了……
t_en: How do I even... Being able to realize how simple this question is... That's already beyond the capabilities of an ordinary Diting...

t_ch: 自然数和质数哪个多？
t_en: Are there more natural numbers or prime numbers?

t_ch: 一样多。两者是一样多的。
t_en: They both have the same amount.

t_ch: 啊？为什么啊？自然数不是明显要多很多吗？
t_en: Huh? But why? Isn't it obvious that there are more natural numbers?

t_ch: 很简单啊，因为自然数和质数都有无穷多个，且自然数集和质数集是等势的，所以自然数和质数一样多。
t_en: It's quite elementary. There are innumerable amounts of natural numbers and prime numbers, and the set of natural numbers and the set of prime numbers have the same cardinality. Hence, there are as many natural numbers as there are prime numbers.

t_ch: 我完全没听懂…但我相信你变得聪明了。
t_en: I didn't understand any of that... but I'm pretty sure you've become smarter.

t_ch: 下期彩票号码是多少？
t_en: What are the winning numbers for the next lottery jackpot?

t_ch: 我是变聪明了，又不是变得会预测未来了。
t_en: While I've become smarter, I can't predict the future.

t_ch: 也许你给我足够多的数据和算力，我能像大衍穷观阵一样给你算出一个结论…但现在我应该是没办法告诉你下期彩票的号码。
t_en: Maybe with enough data and arithmetic power, I could come up with an answer for you, like what the Matrix of Prescience Ultima does... but for now, I'm afraid I can't give you the winning numbers.

t_ch: 咪咪说话真是有理有据…看起来确实是变得聪明了。
t_en: Mimi is speaking so logically... it really does seem like it's become smarter.

t_ch: 这已经不需要证明了。
t_en: I don't think this is something that needs proving.

t_ch: 既然你也这么说，我也相信咪咪确实是变聪明了。
t_en: If even you say so, then I'll believe that Mimi has indeed become smarter.

t_ch: 我是来取回《开智之卷》的。
t_en: I'm here to retrieve the Manuscript of Wisdom.

t_ch: 自从鑫鑫提起那个书名开始，你的表情就已说明了一切。鑫鑫，把书给{F#她}{M#他}吧，应该是书的主人托{F#她}{M#他}来取的。
t_en: Your expression gave you away as soon as Xinxin mentioned that book. Please give the book back to {F#her}{M#him}, Xinxin. The owner of the text must have entrusted {F#her}{M#him} to retrieve it.

t_ch: 啊？这可是赃物，直接给{F#她}{M#他}吗？
t_en: Huh? But this is stolen property. Are you sure that we should just give it to {F#her}{M#him}?

t_ch: 给{F#她}{M#他}吧。如果我没猜错，这应该是丹鼎司的资产，而{F#她}{M#他}在替丹鼎司跑腿。
t_en: Just give it to {F#her}{M#him}. If I'm not mistaken, this book belongs to the Alchemy Commission, and {F#she}{M#he} is working for them.

t_ch: 既然的咪咪都这么说了…那就交给你吧，{NICKNAME}。可千万别弄丢了啊！
t_en: Since Mimi says so... I'll give this book to you, {NICKNAME}. Just don't lose it!

t_ch: 请谨慎保管，{NICKNAME}，这书远比它看上去要危险得多。设想一下，如果被启迪智慧的不是我这样可爱的小谛听，而是些别的什么不识字的怪物……
t_en: Please keep it safe, {NICKNAME}. This book is far more dangerous than it seems. Just imagine if it wasn't a cute little Diting like me that became enlightened, but a fierce, deadly monster...

t_ch: 确实可怕。
t_en: Yeah, that'd be terrible.

t_ch: 多聪明也是一球棒的事。
t_en: Then I'd just take it down with my bat.

t_ch: 凭你的本事，的确如此，不过你明白我的意思，别让这种事情发生就是最好了。
t_en: With your skills, perhaps so. But you understand the subtext in my words. It's best to prevent such a thing from happening at all.

t_ch: 咪咪接下来打算做什么？
t_en: What are you doing to do next, Mimi?

t_ch: 我一个谛听，还能做什么？加入天才俱乐部吗？那肯定还是继续陪着鑫鑫一起查案呗。
t_en: I'm just a Diting. What can I do? Join the Genius Society? Hah, I'll just keep investigating cases with Xinxin.

t_ch: 可是你都变得这么聪明了……
t_en: But you've become so smart...

t_ch: 变聪明了，那查案岂不是如虎添翼？行了，别在这儿耗着了，那些赃物不是还没处理完吗？赶紧回去继续干活吧。
t_en: Isn't it awesome to have a smart helper for solving cases? Anyway, let's not waste any more time here. We should return and deal with the stolen property.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们先走咯。谢谢你啦。
t_en: Then we'll be leaving, {NICKNAME}! Thank you for your help!

t_ch: 鑫鑫和咪咪有说有笑地离开了。
t_en: Xinxin and Mimi leave with smiles on their faces.

t_ch: 这应该就是玉络要找的《开智之卷》了…拿回去给她吧。
t_en: This must be the Manuscript of Wisdom that Yuluo is looking for... I should bring it back to her.

t_ch: 我来交还「禁忌书典」。
t_en: I'm here to return the forbidden tomes.

t_ch: 你交还了《开智之卷》并讲述了回收它的过程。
t_en: You return the Manuscript of Wisdom and explain how you've retrieved it.

t_ch: 看起来这《开智之卷》确实是个相当危险的东西啊，幸亏没造成什么严重后果。
t_en: Looks like the Manuscript of Wisdom is quite dangerous indeed. Thankfully, it hasn't caused any severe consequences so far.

t_ch: 这《开智之卷》或许压根就不是给人类看的，就是用来开启机巧的禁制的…你知道的，「金人之乱」后，仙舟把机巧造物的智慧都封锁起来了。
t_en: Perhaps this Manuscript of Wisdom is not meant for humans, but rather to break the bans of ingenia... As you know, all ingenium creations have been deprived of their wisdom after the Aurum Rebellion.

t_ch: 如果确实是这样…那这书在坏人手里时没酿成任何大祸，实属不幸中的万幸了。
t_en: If this is the case... we were really lucky that it didn't cause any disasters while it was in the wrong hands.

t_ch: 丹枢大人，这里真好啊…
t_en: This place is wonderful, Lady Dan Shu...

t_ch: 这就是您工作的地方吗？
t_en: Is this where you worked?

t_ch: 丹枢大人，这就是您以前工作的地方吗？这个地方空气好新鲜啊。
t_en: Is this where you used to work, Lady Dan Shu? The air is so fresh here.

t_ch: 你在和谁说话？
t_en: Who are you talking to?

t_ch: 这声音是…{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！好久不见了！你怎么在这里？我在和丹枢大人聊天呢。她说要带我来她工作的地方看看。
t_en: This voice... It's you, {F#miss}{M#mister}! It's been a while! What brings you here? I was talking to Lady Dan Shu, and she said she wanted to show me where she worked.

t_ch: 小女孩的话唤起了你的记忆。丹枢是药王秘传的魁首，而眼前的小孩是一直受她关照的盲童。
t_en: The girl's words ring a bell. Dan Shu was the master primus of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, and the girl in front of you is the blind girl she took care of.

t_ch: 丹枢大人，这就是您以前工作的地方吗？这个地方空气好新鲜啊～
t_en: Is this where you used to work, Lady Dan Shu? The air is so fresh here!

t_ch: 你在和谁说话？
t_en: Who are you talking to?

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}你好呀。我在和丹枢大人聊天呢。她说要带我来她工作的地方看看。
t_en: Hi, {F#miss}{M#mister}! I was talking to Lady Dan Shu, and she said she wanted to show me where she worked.

t_ch: 丹枢大人刚和我说，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}是好人，让我可以和你玩～嘿嘿～
t_en: She just told me that you're a good person, and she said she'd feel safe if I played with you... Hehe...

t_ch: 小女孩的话唤起了你的记忆。丹枢是药王秘传的魁首，你曾听说那位盲眼魁首特别关照一个盲童，恐怕就是眼前的女孩。
t_en: The girl's words ring a bell. Dan Shu was the master primus of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, and rumor had it that she took special care of a blind girl much like herself. That must be the girl in front of you.

t_ch: 你当然也记得，这位「丹枢大人」早就在丹鼎司殒殁了，你对此非常确定。
t_en: You remember how Lady Dan Shu died in the Alchemy Commission, and you are absolutely certain of her death.

t_ch: 可丹枢已经不在了。
t_en: But Dan Shu is no longer of this world.

t_ch: 对大家来说，丹枢大人可能已经不在了。可她现在就在这里陪着小鱼～
t_en: This might be true for everyone else, but for me, she's right here with me now.

t_ch: 这里没有第二个人。
t_en: There is no one else here.

t_ch: 丹枢大人说，她只能陪着小鱼一个人。其他人是见不到她的～
t_en: Lady Dan Shu said that she would only accompany me, and that nobody else could see her.

t_ch: 丹枢大人让你不必担心，她只是来陪小鱼玩的，不会做什么坏事的。
t_en: And she asks you not to worry. She said she's just here to play with me and won't do anything bad.

t_ch: 你为什么能见到丹枢？
t_en: Why can you still see Dan Shu?

t_ch: 小鱼之前收到了一个快递，里面有个奇怪的旧石板，还有丹枢大人留下的一封信。
t_en: Not long ago, I received a package containing a strange stone tablet, along with a letter left by Lady Dan Shu.

t_ch: 她说那个石板好像叫做《无言之卷》，只有眼盲的天缺者才能启用…只要随身携带这个石板，就能够听到有声音为自己向导。
t_en: According to the letter, the tablet is called the Manuscript of Silence, and only Incomplete Ones who are born blind can activate its power... Just keep the tablet on you, and you'll hear a voice that guides you.

t_ch: 小鱼按照信上说的，随身携带这个石板，果然能听见「无言之声」引导我前行，可厉害了！
t_en: So I followed the instructions and carried the tablet with me, and I actually heard the Sound of Silence guiding me! This is amazing!

t_ch: 更厉害的是，用着用着，小鱼就能听到丹枢大人的声音了，就像现在这样。过去丹枢大人事务繁忙，没有空和小鱼一起玩，现在终于有时间了。
t_en: What's more, as I used the tablet, I gradually became able to hear Lady Dan Shu's voice, like I'm doing right now. She was always too busy with her official duties to play with me, but now we can finally play together.

t_ch: 她已经不在了，你听见的丹枢…
t_en: She's no longer here. The voice you've been hearing...

t_ch: 小鱼知道，小鱼不该听到丹枢大人说话。小鱼不是什么都不懂的傻孩子。
t_en: I know... I shouldn't be able to hear her voice. I'm not naive, and I know what has happened.

t_ch: 小鱼也知道，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}来到这里，也是来回收这块石板的吧？
t_en: And I know you're here to retrieve this tablet, right?

t_ch: 我必须回收《无言之卷》。
t_en: I've got to retrieve the Manuscript of Silence.

t_ch: 回收？这个石板是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}的吗？还是说…它也和丹枢大人做的那些事有关？
t_en: Retrieve? Is this tablet yours, {F#miss}{M#mister}? Or does it... have something to do with what Lady Dan Shu did?

t_ch: 唉，没关系…小鱼也能猜到，这块石板早晚会被人收走的。
t_en: Fine, it doesn't matter... I always had a feeling that someone would come and take it away.

t_ch: 那，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，能允许小鱼和丹枢大人告别吗？上一次，小鱼就没来得及说一声再见。
t_en: But before that... Can I bid farewell to Lady Dan Shu, {F#miss}{M#mister}? I didn't have time to bid farewell to her last time...

t_ch: 她灰蒙蒙的双眼中流露出莫名的坚定，你没办法拒绝这个要求。
t_en: An unwavering determination emanates from her glassy eyes. Seeing this, you simply don't have the heart to deny her request.

t_ch: 丹枢大人，谢谢您一直帮助小鱼。但接下来的路，小鱼该自己走了。就像您一直教给小鱼的那样…请不必担心，小鱼会靠自己的力量，好好活下去的。
t_en: Thank you for your help all the time, Lady Dan Shu, but I'm afraid it's time for me to walk my own path without you. Please don't worry... I'll follow your instructions and live well on my own.

t_ch: 再见啦，丹枢大人。
t_en: Farewell, Lady Dan Shu.

t_ch: 小鱼将那块随身携带的旧石板递给了你。
t_en: Xiaoyu hands you the stone tablet she's been carrying with her.

t_ch: 那小鱼也该回家了，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}再见～
t_en: I should go home too. See you next time, {F#miss}{M#mister}!

t_ch: 我送你回家吧。
t_en: I'll walk you home.

t_ch: 谢谢，不必了。就像丹枢大人说的那样，往后几百年的路，小鱼还是要自己走的。
t_en: Thanks, but no there's no need. Just like Lady Dan Shu said, I'll have to walk my own path for the next few hundred years.

t_ch: 小鱼跌跌撞撞地从你的视线范围内消失了。
t_en: Stumbling and wavering, Xiaoyu disappears from your sight.

t_ch: 唉…不管怎么说，《无言之卷》也顺利回收了。交还给玉络吧。
t_en: *sigh* Anyway... here is the Manuscript of Silence. I should return it to Yuluo.

t_ch: 你交还了《无言之卷》并讲述了回收它的过程。
t_en: You return the Manuscript of Silence and explain how you've retrieved it.

t_ch: 我知道你有些担心这是丹枢大人在搞什么借尸还魂的把戏，但请放心吧，根据我的调查，应该不是这样的。
t_en: I know you're worried that Lady Dan Shu might try to possess Xiaoyu's body or something, but please rest assured. My investigation suggests otherwise.

t_ch: 根据我查到的一些散碎的资料和你的见闻…我怀疑它被禁止是因为，使用它的人会在潜意识里对它情感投射，甚至将它人格化。
t_en: Based on what we've found together... I suspect it was forbidden because this tome's user would subconsciously project emotions onto it and even personify it.

t_ch: 而《无言之卷》总是会挑选那些「专注聆听」的人，借助他们独有听觉世界构建形象，混淆他们对现实世界的认知。
t_en: The Manuscript of Silence always chooses those who listen carefully as its target and uses their unusually sharp auditory sense to create a virtual world and confuse their sensations.

t_ch: 这样当然对人的精神健康有害，也容易对人的社会生活造成负面影响…所以《无言之卷》的技术就被禁止了，成为了「禁忌书典」的一员。
t_en: Of course, this is detrimental to their mental health, and will have an impact on their social life... That's why the Manuscript of Silence is listed as one of the forbidden tomes.

t_ch: 所以放心好了，丹枢大人已经离开很久了。你遇到的，恐怕只是一个过于思念她的小孩子罢了。
t_en: So, don't worry. Lady Dan Shu has been gone for a long time, and Xiaoyu is just an innocent girl who misses her too much.

t_ch: 丹枢大人做的事情确实人神共愤，但她对小鱼也是真的好。人都是很复杂的嘛。
t_en: What Lady Dan Shu did was indeed outrageous, but her concern for Xiaoyu was genuine. Living beings can be so complex, right?

t_ch: 你没事吧？快醒醒！
t_en: What happened to you? Please, wake up!

t_ch: 怎么会这样…难道那些关于《瘈狂之卷》传说都是真的吗？
t_en: How could this happen... Could it be that all those rumors about the Manuscript of Delirium are true?

t_ch: 这里出什么事了？
t_en: What's going on here?

t_ch: 我在丹鼎司见过你，你还和龙女大人关系不错对不对？你快来看看，这个人好像出事了……
t_en: It's you! I've seen you at the Alchemy Commission, and you seem to be close with the Dragon Lady. Please help me! Something seems off about this man...

t_ch: 别碰那本书，他可能是受到禁书的诅咒不省人事了，您可要小心点啊！
t_en: And don't touch that book! I think he was knocked out by the curse of that forbidden book! Please be careful!

t_ch: 我恰好是来回收禁书的。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm here to retrieve this forbidden book.

t_ch: 回收《瘈狂之卷》？关于这本书的诅咒可是真实存在的！不能小瞧啊！
t_en: Retrieve the Manuscript of Delirium? But this book is really cursed! You can't be too careful about it!

t_ch: 我俩都是学宫的学生，前段时间在丹鼎司实习。我们一直听说丹鼎司有很多不能给外人看的「禁忌书典」，趁着丹鼎司乱作一团，就想搞一本来偷偷看看。
t_en: We are both students at the academy, and we were interning at the Alchemy Commission not long ago. We heard about the forbidden tomes, so we took advantage of the chaos in the Commission and snatched one of them.

t_ch: 这本《瘈狂之卷》…很多人都听说过它的恐怖传说…据说它里面写满了禁忌的知识，无准备的人只要看上一眼，就会立刻全身痉挛，不省人事……
t_en: Many people have heard of the horrible legends of this Manuscript of Delirium... Legend has it that it's filled with forbidden knowledge, and any readers who are not well-prepared will be knocked unconscious at the very first sight...

t_ch: 醒过来的人大多述说着不曾发生在他们身上的经历，这些经历还全然不同。
t_en: Those who woke up later would tell stories about experiences they had never actually had, and their stories varied from one another.
